name: Paella a La Basquaise
recipeIngredient: 1 lb Fresh mussels, thoroughly rinsed and scrubbed
18 small Hard-shell clams, thoroughly rinsed and scrubbed
2 lbs Fresh halibut, cut into bite-size cubes
1 lb Fresh shrimp, peeled with tails left on
4 large Ripe tomatoes, peeled, seeded and chopped
3 cups Hot chicken stock, up to 4 cups Stock
1 cup Shelled peas (blanched in boiling water for 5 mins)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350 degrees.
2. Wash the rice thoroughly under cold running water and drain. Reserve.
3. Wash both the mussels and clams thoroughly until all sand has been removed. Do not soak them in cold water. Dry the fish pieces thoroughly with paper towels and season with salt and pepper.
4. In a large skillet, heat 1/4 cup olive oil. When hot, add the fish cubes and brown on all sides. Remove to a side dish and reserve.
5. To the remaining fat in the skillet, add the shrimp and cook over medium heat until they turn bright pink. Season with salt and white pepper. Remove cooked shrimp to a side dish and reserve.
6. Add 2 tablespoons olive oil to the skillet. Add onion, garlic, and red pepper slices and cook until onions are very soft and lightly browned. Do not burn the mixture.
7. Add tomatoes, prosciutto, chilies, and saffron. Season with salt, pepper, oregano, and basil. Bring to a boil and cook, stirring constantly and scraping the bottom of the pan well, until most of the tomato juice has evaporated.
8. Add rice and 3 1/2 cups chicken stock. Bring mixture to a boil again and cook over medium heat until half of the broth is absorbed.
9. Pour half the rice mixture into an earthenware casserole. Make a layer of fish cubes and chorizo, then top with remaining rice. Cover the casserole and bake for 25 minutes.
10. Uncover the casserole and fold shrimp and peas lightly into the rice. Cover again and continue baking for 10 to 15 minutes or until rice is tender and all broth has been absorbed.
11. If rice seems dry but still crisp, add remaining chicken stock. Uncover the casserole and bury clams and mussels in the rice. Cover and cook for another 10 to 15 minutes or until shells of both mussels and clams have opened.
12. Discard any unopened mussels or clams.
13. Garnish with pimiento strips and lemon quarters. Serve directly from casserole.
